content.problemStatement: Given the roots of two binary trees root and subRoot, return true if there is a subtree of root with the same structure and node values of subRoot and false otherwise.
constraints: 1 <= number of nodes in the tree <= 2000
The input trees will not be empty
All node values will be unique
content.inputFormat: The input will contain two binary trees represented as a root node. Each node will have an integer value and references to its left and right children.
content.outputFormat: A boolean value indicating whether there is a subtree of the first tree that matches the second tree.

hidden.label: tricky case / empty-like / negative if allowed

solution.approach: Use a recursive approach to traverse the main tree and check for a match with the subtree at each node.
solution.timeComplexity: O(n * m) where n is the number of nodes in the main tree and m is the number of nodes in the subtree
solution.spaceComplexity: O(h) where h is the height of the main tree
